title: ranunculus ficaria subsp. grandiflorus
intro: Ranunculus ficaria subsp. grandiflorus, also known as Lesser Celandine, is a perennial flowering plant with yellow flowers and heart-shaped leaves that grows in woodland areas and along streams. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, making it useful for treating skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Lesser Celandine include its ability to reduce inflammation, soothe digestive issues, and support the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and leaves are often dried or made into a tincture to create remedies that can be used to treat a range of health issues.
